japanese_name: ChargeSPOT モバイルバッテリーレンタル
description: ChargeSPOT power bank rental. The konbini save button for a dying phone. Grab one at the kiosk, return it somewhere else, keep Google Maps alive.
notes: [VENUE_CATEGORY: konbini mobile battery; ChargeSPOT rental, USB-C power bank, Lightning cable battery]

japanese_name: モチクリーム大福
description: Mochi Cream daifuku. Soft mochi, cream fillings, fruit versions for people who treat the station fridge like a dessert museum. Easy omiyage when cake feels too serious.
notes: [AMBIGUOUS: MOCHICREAM fruit daifuku, cream daifuku, konbini cream daifuku]

japanese_name: 雪見だいふく
description: Yukimi Daifuku. Tiny vanilla ice cream pucks wrapped in soft mochi. The freezer-aisle snack Japan keeps emotionally attached to.

japanese_name: もみじ饅頭
description: Momiji manju. Maple-leaf cake with anko inside. Hiroshima's souvenir energy, shaped like fall and boxed for train-station gifting.

japanese_name: ムヒS
description: Muhi S. Anti-itch cream for mosquito bites and mystery skin rage. Summer in Japan keeps one in the bag for a reason.

japanese_name: ムヒS
description: Muhi S insect bite cream. Same red-and-white tube, same cold tingle, same mosquito apology tour. Drugstore shelf, beach bag, festival bag.

japanese_name: 無印良品 アロマストーン
description: MUJI Aroma Stone. Ceramic scent pebble for essential oil, no flame, no plug. Hotel-room vibe control without packing a tiny spa.
notes: [AMBIGUOUS: MUJI Aroma Stone, MUJI ultrasonic aroma diffuser, MUJI portable aroma diffuser]

japanese_name: 無印良品 ポリエステル仕分けケース圧縮タイプ
description: MUJI Compression Packing Case. Packing cube with a zipper that bullies clothes flatter. Suitcase chaos loses a round.

english_name: MUJI Sensitive Skin Toner High Moisture
japanese_name: 無印良品 敏感肌用化粧水 高保湿
description: MUJI Sensitive Skin Toner. Plain, gentle-feeling lotion with the MUJI beige-label calm. Drugstore-glow people use it when their face wants less drama.

japanese_name: 無印良品 PET小分けボトル
description: MUJI travel bottle set. Tiny refill bottles for shampoo, toner, and whatever liquid wants to leak in your pouch. Boring, useful, very airport-security coded.
notes: [VENUE_CATEGORY: MUJI travel containers; PET squeeze bottle, spray bottle, pump bottle, foaming bottle]

japanese_name: 無印良品 ふうっとふくらむネッククッション
description: MUJI inflatable neck cushion. Packs flat, puffs up by breath, saves your neck from the bus-nap shrimp position. Better than hugging your backpack like a gremlin.

japanese_name: 福袋
description: Fukubukuro lucky bags. New Year mystery bundles with either a bargain or a personal attack inside. Department stores, food brands, and Donki all get involved.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: Donki fukubukuro, Starbucks lucky bag, Muji lucky bag, snack lucky bag]

japanese_name: 福砂屋 カステラ
description: Nagasaki castella. Sponge cake with a sugar-crunch bottom. Airport sweet box behavior, but with actual tea-time authority.

japanese_name: 青柳ういろう
description: Nagoya uiro. Chewy steamed rice sweet in tidy blocks. Nagoya souvenir math: simple, soft, oddly addictive.

japanese_name: ダイソー おなまえシール
description: Daiso name label stickers. Waterproof-ish school-label sheets for bags, bottles, and cables. Also perfect for adults pretending their life has systems.

japanese_name: ななチキ
description: Nanachiki. 7-Eleven fried chicken from the hot case, juicy and salty in the correct parking-lot way. Eat it before the wrapper gives up.

japanese_name: ナチュリエ ハトムギ化粧水
description: Naturie Hatomugi Skin Conditioner. Big 500ml watery lotion, light and unfussy. The drugstore bottle people buy when one face toner feels too small.

japanese_name: 熱さまシート
description: Netsusama Cooling Sheets. Stick-on gel sheets for fever foreheads and summer survival. Not medicine, just the cold little rectangle everyone knows.

japanese_name: ニューヨークパーフェクトチーズ
description: New York Perfect Cheese. Gouda langue de chat wrapped around cream and cheese chocolate. Tokyo Station snack hype in a very slim box.

japanese_name: アイコス イルマ i ワン
description: IQOS ILUMA i ONE. Heated tobacco device, not a cute tech souvenir. Adult-only counter item, with Japan rules tourists should read first.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: IQOS ILUMA i, Ploom X Advanced, non-nicotine vape devices, vape liquid; regulated age-restricted product]

japanese_name: じゅわっとジューシー本格肉まん
description: FamilyMart nikuman. Hot-case pork bun with soft dough and juicy filling. Best eaten outside while pretending this counts as lunch.
notes: [VENUE_CATEGORY: konbini hot case; pork bun, black pork bun, pizza-man]

japanese_name: のびーるチーズのコク旨ピザまん
description: FamilyMart pizza-man. Yellow steamed bun with tomato sauce and stretchy cheese. Konbini winter food for people who wanted pizza but met the bun machine first.
notes: [VENUE_CATEGORY: konbini hot case; nikuman, pizza-man, black pork bun]

japanese_name: 人形焼
description: Ningyo-yaki. Little Tokyo cakes shaped like sights and stuffed with anko or cream. Asakusa snack path behavior, in box form.

japanese_name: ニベアクリーム
description: Nivea Cream blue tin. Thick body-and-face cream in the classic blue can. Dry hands, cold weather, one tin that survives every drugstore shelf.

english_name: Nivea UV Super Water Gel SPF50
japanese_name: ニベアUV ウォータージェルSPF50
description: Nivea UV Super Water Gel. SPF50 gel sunscreen that feels more lotion than beach armor. Daily city sunscreen, not the waterproof pool-day one.
